seo.title: Government AI Chatbot AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai chatbot AI agents in government. Covers FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53.

content.intro: Deploying an AI chatbot in a government context carries extraordinary stakes: failures can compromise national security, violate civil liberties, and trigger congressional scrutiny. This checklist operationalizes FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, and the Executive Order on AI requirements specifically for conversational agents that interact with citizens and handle sensitive government data. Every item is designed to be verified, audited, and defensible under federal oversight.

sections.heading: Data Classification & Security Controls
sections.description: Ensure the chatbot architecture enforces strict data boundaries, prevents classified data exposure to external LLMs, and meets FedRAMP and FISMA security baselines before any citizen interaction occurs.

items.label: Classify all data sources ingested into the RAG pipeline
items.description: Tag every document, database, and API feed with its classification level (Unclassified, CUI, Sensitive, Classified). Block any source rated above the system's authorized classification level from entering the retrieval index.

items.label: Enforce a hard architectural barrier preventing classified data from reaching external LLM APIs
items.description: Implement network-level controls, data loss prevention (DLP) rules, and pre-send content inspection to ensure no classified or CUI content is transmitted to third-party model providers such as OpenAI or Anthropic.

items.label: Obtain and maintain a FedRAMP Authorization to Operate (ATO) for all cloud components
items.description: Verify that every infrastructure component—vector database, LLM inference endpoint, memory store, and API gateway—is listed on the FedRAMP Marketplace or covered under an existing agency ATO before go-live.

items.label: Implement NIST 800-53 AC-3 and AC-4 access enforcement on retrieval indexes
items.description: Apply role-based access controls so the chatbot's retrieval queries only surface documents the authenticated user's clearance and need-to-know permit. Enforce information flow policies between security domains.

items.label: Enable end-to-end encryption (TLS 1.3+) for all chatbot traffic and data at rest (AES-256)
items.description: Confirm that conversation payloads, retrieved context chunks, and multi-turn memory stores are encrypted in transit and at rest using FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules.

items.label: Configure session-level PII scrubbing before logging conversation turns
items.description: Deploy a PII detection layer (SSN, DOB, address, case numbers) that redacts or tokenizes sensitive fields in logs, audit trails, and memory stores to comply with Privacy Act of 1974 requirements.

items.label: Conduct a Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) covering multi-turn memory retention
items.description: Document what citizen data is retained across conversation turns, for how long, and under what legal authority. Publish the PIA per OMB Circular A-130 before the chatbot handles live citizen queries.

sections.heading: Algorithmic Accountability & Explainability
sections.description: Federal regulations and the Executive Order on AI require that AI-assisted decisions affecting citizens be explainable, auditable, and appealable. This section ensures the chatbot's intent classification and response generation meet those mandates.

items.label: Complete a formal Algorithmic Impact Assessment (AIA) before production deployment
items.description: Document the chatbot's decision logic, training data sources, known failure modes, and disparate impact risks across demographic groups. Have the AIA reviewed by the agency's AI governance board and legal counsel.

items.label: Implement response provenance tracking linking every chatbot answer to its source documents
items.description: For each RAG-generated response, log which retrieved chunks contributed to the answer, their document IDs, and confidence scores. Make this audit trail available to oversight personnel within 24 hours of a request.

items.label: Build a citizen-facing explanation endpoint for any AI-assisted determination
items.description: When the chatbot informs a citizen of an eligibility decision, benefit status, or regulatory requirement, provide a plain-language explanation of the factors considered and a clear pathway to human review or appeal.

items.label: Instrument intent classification confidence thresholds with mandatory human escalation
items.description: Define confidence score thresholds for the intent classifier. Any query scoring below the threshold on high-stakes intents (benefits denial, legal obligations, enforcement actions) must be routed to a human agent rather than answered autonomously.

items.label: Log all multi-turn conversation sessions with immutable audit trails
items.description: Store complete conversation logs in a WORM (Write Once Read Many) compliant storage system. Logs must include timestamps, user identifiers (anonymized where required), intent classifications, retrieved context, and final responses.

items.label: Establish a bias and fairness testing protocol across protected demographic groups
items.description: Run quarterly evaluations measuring response quality, refusal rates, and accuracy disparities across race, age, disability status, and language proficiency. Document results and remediation actions per EO 13985 equity requirements.

items.label: Define and publish the chatbot's scope of authority in a publicly accessible system card
items.description: Clearly state what decisions the chatbot can and cannot make autonomously, what data it accesses, and how citizens can opt out of AI-assisted interactions. Post this on the agency's AI inventory per EO 14110.

sections.heading: Safety Filtering, Hallucination Control & Response Accuracy
sections.description: Government chatbots providing incorrect legal, benefits, or regulatory information can cause direct citizen harm. This section enforces verification controls specific to RAG-based conversational agents in high-stakes public service contexts.

items.label: Deploy a hallucination detection layer that cross-checks responses against retrieved source chunks
items.description: Implement an automated faithfulness scorer (e.g., NLI-based entailment check) that flags responses containing claims not grounded in the retrieved context. Responses exceeding a hallucination risk threshold must be blocked or escalated.

items.label: Implement a multi-stage safety filter pipeline covering harmful content, prompt injection, and jailbreak attempts
items.description: Apply input and output safety classifiers that detect attempts to extract system prompts, bypass content filters, elicit classified information, or generate harmful content. Log all flagged attempts for security review.

items.label: Establish a ground-truth evaluation dataset of 500+ government-specific Q&A pairs for continuous accuracy benchmarking
items.description: Curate a labeled test set covering benefits eligibility, regulatory requirements, and agency procedures. Run automated accuracy evaluations on every model or retrieval index update, with a minimum passing threshold defined in the system's ATO documentation.

items.label: Configure retrieval freshness controls to prevent responses based on outdated regulations or policy
items.description: Tag all RAG index documents with effective dates and expiration dates. Implement a staleness filter that excludes superseded regulations, expired guidance, and outdated policy documents from retrieval results.

items.label: Implement conversation coherence monitoring to detect context drift in multi-turn sessions
items.description: Deploy a session-level coherence checker that detects when the chatbot's responses contradict earlier statements in the same conversation or lose track of established context, triggering a session reset or human handoff.

items.label: Define and enforce topic boundary restrictions preventing the chatbot from responding outside its authorized scope
items.description: Implement an out-of-scope classifier that redirects queries on unauthorized topics (e.g., legal advice, medical diagnosis, classified programs) to appropriate resources rather than attempting an answer.

items.label: Conduct red team adversarial testing simulating citizen manipulation and data extraction attempts
items.description: Engage an independent red team to attempt prompt injection, social engineering, PII extraction, and misinformation elicitation before go-live and after every major model update. Document findings in the system's Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M).

items.label: Establish a citizen feedback loop for flagging incorrect or harmful responses
items.description: Provide a visible 'Report an Issue' mechanism in the chat interface. Route flagged responses to a human reviewer within 4 business hours, and track resolution metrics in the agency's AI performance dashboard.

sections.heading: Accessibility, Incident Response & Governance
sections.description: Section 508 compliance, defined incident response procedures, and ongoing governance structures are non-negotiable for federal AI deployments. This section ensures the chatbot is accessible to all citizens and that failures are contained and reported appropriately.

items.label: Conduct a Section 508 conformance audit of the chatbot interface against WCAG 2.1 AA standards
items.description: Test the chat UI with screen readers (JAWS, NVDA), keyboard-only navigation, and color contrast analyzers. Verify that all interactive elements, error messages, and response content meet Section 508 technical standards before launch.

items.label: Provide multilingual support covering the agency's top 10 non-English languages per LEP obligations
items.description: Implement language detection and response generation in languages required by Executive Order 13166 (Limited English Proficiency). Validate translation accuracy with native speakers for high-stakes content categories.

items.label: Define a FISMA-compliant incident response plan specific to AI chatbot failure scenarios
items.description: Document response procedures for: data breach via conversation logs, hallucinated legal guidance causing citizen harm, prompt injection leading to unauthorized data access, and model availability outages. Assign named owners and escalation timelines for each scenario.

items.label: Establish a Continuous Monitoring program per NIST 800-137 covering chatbot-specific metrics
items.description: Define and automate monitoring for: response accuracy drift, safety filter bypass rates, PII detection failures, retrieval latency, and hallucination scores. Set alert thresholds that trigger automated shutdown or human review.

items.label: Register the chatbot in the agency's AI use case inventory per EO 14110 reporting requirements
items.description: Submit the required AI use case documentation to OMB, including system purpose, data inputs, decision authority, risk level, and mitigation measures. Update the inventory within 30 days of any material system change.

items.label: Establish a quarterly AI governance review board meeting to assess chatbot performance and compliance
items.description: Convene agency CISO, privacy officer, legal counsel, and program owners quarterly to review accuracy metrics, incident reports, bias testing results, and regulatory changes. Document decisions and action items with assigned owners.

items.label: Implement a model change management process requiring re-authorization for significant updates
items.description: Define what constitutes a significant change (new LLM version, retrieval index overhaul, new data sources) and require a security impact analysis and abbreviated ATO review before deploying changes to production.

notes.note: Every cloud service component in the chatbot stack—including the LLM inference API, vector database, and session memory store—must hold a FedRAMP Authorization or be covered under the agency's existing ATO. FISMA requires annual security assessments and continuous monitoring; chatbot-specific controls must be documented in the System Security Plan (SSP) and tested during the annual assessment cycle.

The chatbot must implement applicable controls from NIST 800-53 Rev 5, with particular attention to: AC-3 (Access Enforcement) for retrieval authorization, AU-2/AU-12 (Audit Events/Audit Record Generation) for conversation logging, SI-10 (Information Input Validation) for prompt injection prevention, and the newly added PT (Personally Identifiable Information Processing) control family for citizen data handling.

notes.regulation: Executive Order 14110 on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
notes.note: EO 14110 requires federal agencies to designate a Chief AI Officer, maintain an AI use case inventory, and conduct impact assessments for AI systems affecting rights or safety. The chatbot must be registered in the agency inventory, and any use case involving benefits determination, legal interpretation, or enforcement guidance must undergo a rights-impacting AI review with documented mitigation measures.

All federal-facing electronic and information technology, including AI chatbot interfaces, must conform to Section 508 standards (36 CFR Part 1194) and WCAG 2.1 AA. This includes the chat interface, any visual response formatting, error messages, and file attachments. Agencies must obtain a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT) from vendors and conduct independent conformance testing before deployment.

cta.heading: Automate Your Government Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your government AI chatbot against FedRAMP, FISMA, and EO 14110 requirements—automatically flagging compliance gaps, tracking audit trails, and generating ATO-ready documentation before your next assessment.
